name: json-validator
description: Validate, format, and fix JSON data. Use this skill when working with JSON files, API responses, or configuration files that need validation or formatting.
capabilities: Validate JSON syntax with precise line numbers
Fix common JSON errors (trailing commas, single quotes)
Convert JSON to YAML, TOML, CSV, and XML formats
Analyze JSON structure and report schema information
Provide clear error messages with suggested corrections
author: Alon2904
securitySummary: This is a pure prompt-based skill with no code execution, file access, or network capabilities. The skill provides only instructions for AI agents to help users validate, format, and fix JSON data. All 41 static findings are false positives caused by keyword-based pattern detection misinterpreting markdown documentation as security threats.
valueStatement: JSON syntax errors cause bugs and integration failures. This skill validates JSON structure, fixes common errors like trailing commas and unquoted keys, and formats data cleanly.
limitations: Cannot directly read or write files on your system
Does not handle extremely large JSON files efficiently

useCases.title: Debug API responses
useCases.description: Quickly identify and fix JSON syntax errors in API responses or configuration files.

useCases.title: Convert data formats
useCases.description: Transform JSON data into YAML, TOML, or other formats for different systems.

useCases.title: Validate data structures
useCases.description: Check JSON payloads for correct structure and identify missing or invalid fields.

promptTemplates.title: Basic validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate this JSON and show me any errors with line numbers: [PASTE JSON HERE]

promptTemplates.title: Fix errors
promptTemplates.prompt: Fix the errors in this JSON and show me the corrected version: [PASTE JSON HERE]

promptTemplates.title: Format output
promptTemplates.prompt: Format this JSON with 2-space indentation: [PASTE JSON HERE]

promptTemplates.title: Convert format
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this JSON to [YAML/TOML/CSV/XML]: [PASTE JSON HERE]

bestPractices: Validate before formatting to avoid masking syntax errors
Include line numbers when reporting errors for easier debugging
Preserve original data types and avoid unnecessary string conversions
antiPatterns: Formatting invalid JSON without first fixing syntax errors
Converting JSON to other formats without validating first
Using single quotes instead of double quotes in JSON

faq.question: What JSON validation rules does this skill check?
faq.answer: It checks for syntax errors, missing or extra commas, unclosed brackets, unquoted keys, trailing commas, single quotes, and invalid values.

faq.question: Does this skill support JSON Schema validation?
faq.answer: No, this skill validates basic JSON syntax and structure but does not validate against JSON Schema specifications.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to format large JSON files?
faq.answer: The skill works with JSON of any size, but extremely large files may be slow to process in a single request.

faq.question: What formatting options are available?
faq.answer: The default uses 2-space indentation. You can optionally request alphabetical key sorting or minimal whitespace.

faq.question: Is my data sent to any external servers?
faq.answer: No, this is a prompt-based skill that runs entirely within your AI tool. No data leaves your system.

faq.question: How does this compare to online JSON validators?
faq.answer: This skill works offline, provides immediate feedback, integrates with your workflow, and can fix errors automatically.
